seo_title: Mitigating Artifact Drift in Agent Benchmark Generation
meta_description: A task-generation pipeline formalizes domain experts' specifications of business workflows into constraint optimization programs to produce auditable…
body_html:
AI agents are beginning to complete valuable, long-horizon business operations tasks, but training and evaluation environments for enterprise work still struggle to balance realism, verifiability, and scale.

Anchor introduces a task-generation pipeline that formalizes domain experts' specifications of business workflows into constraint optimization programs. The pipeline jointly produces a natural-language instruction, environment configuration, solver-certified ground-truth solution, and state-based verifier. With Anchor, altering parameters yields new tasks with controlled difficulty and known optimal solutions, producing harness-agnostic environments whose rewards depend solely on end-state business correctness.

ERP-Bench: a benchmark of 300 long-horizon tasks spanning procurement and manufacturing workflows in a production-grade ERP system, demonstrates the effectiveness of Anchor in producing auditable evaluation environments for economically valuable agent work. ParameterRealized DifficultyOptimal Solution RateGeneration ParametersPredicts Realized DifficultyFrontier Models Satisfy Explicit Task Constraints in 26.1% of TrialsOptimal Solution RateReach Fully Optimal Solution in Only 17.4% of Trials

The study highlights the importance of developing auditable evaluation environments for economically valuable agent work, and Anchor provides a concrete recipe for achieving this goal. Anchor is released as an open-source task generator, along with the ERP-Bench dataset, to facilitate further research and development in this area.

source.title: Anchor: Mitigating Artifact Drift in Agent Benchmark Generation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new tool called Anchor to mitigate "artifact drift" in generating training environments for AI agents, which can lead to unsolvable or inconsistent tasks. The system formalizes domain experts' specifications into constraint optimization programs, producing a natural-language instruction, environment configuration, and verifier. By applying this tool, the authors created ERP-Bench, a benchmark of 300 long-horizon tasks in procurement and manufacturing workflows, demonstrating its effectiveness in building auditable evaluation environments for economically valuable agent work.
